title: Felting with pre-felt batt
subtitle: Using commercially prepared prefelt sheets as a base or inlay material
excerpt: Pre-felt batt is lightly fulled wool that has been carded into a flat sheet. It holds its shape and can be cut to silhouettes, but it is not yet bonded firmly enough to withstand washing. Wet-felted into a panel or vessel as an inlay, it gives clean colour boundaries and precise shapes that freeform roving layering cannot easily produce.
sourceNotes: Pre-felt inlay technique is standard in contemporary wet-felting instruction. Technique is observation-led.

content.text: to a clean shape, like a leaf, a circle, or a triangle. Lay it on a base panel before felting, as an
. The shape stays crisp in the finished piece. Pick a colour to match or contrast with the base. The two layers felt together during wet-felting.

items.name: Pre-felt batt, contrasting colour to the base
Merino roving for the base layer

content.text: Lay out the base roving in two crossed layers on the bamboo mat, roughly A4 size. Keep the layers thin and even.
Step 2: Cut and place the pre-felt shapes

Cut the pre-felt batt into the desired shapes with sharp scissors. The pre-felt holds its cut edge cleanly, which is its main advantage over loose roving. Place the cut shapes on the base layer in the desired arrangement. Allow a slight overlap at the edges where shapes touch one another.

Cover the panel with the felting net. Drizzle hot soapy water over the surface and rub gently in circular motions for 5 to 7 minutes. Lift the net and check that the inlay shapes have bonded to the base. The edges of each inlay shape should be merging with the base layer rather than lifting.

Remove the net. Rub firmly for a further 10 to 15 minutes. Roll the panel in the bamboo mat and full by applying hand pressure along the roll. Unfurl, rotate 90 degrees, re-roll, and full again. Rinse in clean cool water. Dry flat.

To make pre-felt from roving: lay three thin crossed layers as for a flat panel. Wet down and rub very gently for 3 to 4 minutes until the surface holds together when lifted. Stop before the fibres begin to felt densely. This lightly matted sheet is pre-felt. Dry flat and store flat; it can be cut and used for weeks.

attrs.intro: One common problem with pre-felt inlay.

items.fix: In the first 5 minutes, rub only gently using the fingertip pressure. Once the inlay has bonded and the net has been removed, the shapes will not shift further.
items.cause: The pre-felt was placed on an uneven base layer, or rubbing was too vigorous before the inlay bonded.
items.symptom: Inlay shapes shift position during rubbing

attrs.heading: Common problems
